title: rosa sepium var. albiflora
intro: Rosa sepium var. albiflora, also known as White Dog Rose, is a variety of wild rose native to North America, characterized by its fragrant white flowers and edible hips. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be applied topically to soothe wounds and skin irritations. Consuming the hips of the White Dog Rose has been reported to have various health benefits, including acting as a natural diuretic, reducing fever, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in herbal remedies for its medicinal properties.
